direction.description: CoAP Token and endpoint context preserve request-response association.

spec_ref.quote_or_summary: The Token is used to match a response with a request.

statement: If a Token is used to match CoAP requests and responses, implementations must not truncate, confuse, or incorrectly reuse Tokens across endpoint or request contexts.
condition: A CoAP endpoint receives responses, ACK/RST messages, or notifications involving Tokens and endpoint context.
expected_behavior: Matching uses the full Token and relevant endpoint/request context.
violation_pattern: A response or state transition is matched using a truncated or stale Token or the wrong endpoint context.

review.review_notes: Good constraint generation seed even without a specific CVE; validate against libcoap/FreeCoAP behavior.
